critique.summary: OpenTTD will no longer be available as a standalone game on Steam, but can be purchased as part of a bundle with Transport Tycoon Deluxe. Existing players will not be affected, and the game remains free, although new players must buy the bundle to access it.
mainPoints: OpenTTD is now available only as part of a bundle with Transport Tycoon Deluxe on Steam.
Existing players will continue to receive updates and can re-download the game without changes.
critique.truthfulness: The article accurately describes the changes to OpenTTD's availability on Steam and clarifies that existing players will not be affected. It also correctly states that the game remains free, but new players must purchase the bundle to access it. The information aligns with current distribution practices.
considerations: The article does not mention the potential impact on new players who may find the bundle pricing less appealing.
It does not address how this change might affect the game's community or player engagement.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the implications of OpenTTD's availability on Steam and its relationship with Atari's Transport Tycoon Deluxe. Comments reflect a mix of optimism about the arrangement and critiques of the games involved.

comments.author: ndiddy
comments.text: Obviously having OpenTTD available for free on Steam would jeopardize Atari's paid rerelease of Transport Tycoon Deluxe, so I think this is a good com...
comments.analysis: This comment provides a well-reasoned perspective on the financial implications of the arrangement and acknowledges the contributions of the OpenTTD team.

comments.author: ocdtrekkie
comments.text: This seems quite positive to me: Clearly the rightsholders are not being total jerks since they're happy to allow an OpenTTD bundle, and the or...
comments.analysis: The comment offers a positive view of the situation, suggesting that the rights holders are being reasonable, which aligns with the overall sentiment.

comments.author: xyzzy_plugh
comments.text: It would be nice if someone could provide some explanation as to why this situation is necessary. Did Atari's lawyers go full tilt?
comments.analysis: This comment raises a valid question about the legal context but lacks depth in exploring the implications of the situation.

comments.author: jjmarr
comments.text:
I like Simutrans more because the cargo and passengers have destinations in mind.

TTD and OpenTTD do not which incentivizes mechanisms to dump...
comments.analysis: While the comment provides a personal preference, it diverges from the main topic and does not directly address the implications of the Steam release.

comments.author: basilikum
comments.text:
OpenTTD with its beyond clunky UI has a special spot in my heart.

comments.analysis: This comment expresses nostalgia but does not contribute significantly to the discussion about the Steam distribution changes.
